req.title: Open savings account (boundary variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-027 mandates that a verified customer can open a savings account with an initial deposit >= $100, with tiered interest auto-assigned, and that deposits < $100 are rejected with HTTP 422. Test design covers: (1) EP positive — valid mid-partition deposit well above minimum to confirm account creation and tier assignment; (2) NEG negative — deposit below minimum to confirm 422 rejection; (3) BVA boundary-min — exact $100 deposit to confirm acceptance at the lower boundary; (4) BVA boundary-below-min — $99.99 to confirm rejection one cent below boundary; (5) STC state transition — verifying the account moves from PENDING to ACTIVE after creation, confirming the state machine is exercised. Each test is self-contained via a verified-customer fixture and uses idempotent cleanup.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit
actions: POST /accounts/savings with verified customer credentials and initial_deposit=500.00
expected: Response body contains interest_tier assigned automatically
trace: EP: mid-partition of valid deposit range (>=100)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit(verified_customer_client, cleanup_account):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer_client["customer_id"],
        "initial_deposit": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = verified_customer_client["session"].post(
        "/accounts/savings", json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "ACTIVE"
    assert "interest_tier" in body
    assert body["interest_tier"] is not None
    cleanup_account(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/savings with verified customer credentials and initial_deposit=50.00
expected: Response body contains error code indicating minimum balance violation
trace: NEG: invalid partition below minimum threshold
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_rejected(verified_customer_client):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer_client["customer_id"],
        "initial_deposit": 50.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = verified_customer_client["session"].post(
        "/accounts/savings", json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "minimum" in error_text or "balance" in error_text or "100" in error_text

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_at_exact_minimum_boundary
actions: POST /accounts/savings with verified customer credentials and initial_deposit=100.00
expected: Response body contains interest_tier (lowest tier expected at boundary)
trace: R-FIN-027: minimum balance of $100 must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_at_exact_minimum_boundary(verified_customer_client, cleanup_account):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer_client["customer_id"],
        "initial_deposit": 100.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = verified_customer_client["session"].post(
        "/accounts/savings", json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "ACTIVE"
    assert "interest_tier" in body
    assert body["interest_tier"] is not None
    cleanup_account(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_one_cent_below_minimum
actions: POST /accounts/savings with verified customer credentials and initial_deposit=99.99
trace: BVA: one unit below minimum boundary (off-by-one guard)

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_state_transitions_to_active
preconditions: Account does not yet exist for this customer session
actions: Verify state transitioned from creation to ACTIVE
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with status = ACTIVE or PENDING
GET returns HTTP 200 with status = ACTIVE
interest_tier present in final GET response
trace: STC: PENDING -> ACTIVE state transition upon successful account opening
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_state_transitions_to_active(verified_customer_client, cleanup_account):
    session = verified_customer_client["session"]
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer_client["customer_id"],
        "initial_deposit": 250.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    create_resp = session.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201, f"Create failed: {create_resp.text}"
    account_id = create_resp.json()["account_id"]

    get_resp = session.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"Fetch failed: {get_resp.text}"
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "ACTIVE"
    assert "interest_tier" in body
    cleanup_account(account_id)

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_unverified_customer_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/savings with unverified customer credentials and initial_deposit=200.00
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden or HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
trace: DT: decision row — valid deposit but unverified customer => rejected
